mapping.a_element: XY model spin configuration and its fluctuations
mapping.b_element: Ising model spin configuration and its fluctuations

mapping.a_element: Binder's cumulant for detecting phase transitions
mapping.b_element: finite-size scaling and reweighting techniques for critical properties

mapping.a_element: vortex density and correlation function in low-temperature approximation
mapping.b_element: improved estimators for measurements in the disordered phase

mapping.a_element: zero-temperature phase transition due to strong spinwave fluctuations
mapping.b_element: finite-temperature phase transition with critical exponents matching regular lattices

brief_justification: While both papers study phase transitions on non-regular lattices using numerical methods (Monte Carlo simulations, finite-size scaling, and correlation functions), the underlying mechanisms differ: Paper A focuses on the XY model's topological defects (vortices) and spinwave fluctuations driving a zero-temperature transition, whereas Paper B examines the Ising model's universality class and critical exponents at a finite-temperature transition, with no shared dynamical or mathematical machinery.
